summary: A proper attacking 4-3-3 under a new U23-style manager — most shots and most crosses per 90, with excellent defensive structure. Could a Moroccan side carry a continent again?
style: 4-3-3 (new manager's U23 style) — most shots per 90, 1v1 wingers & attacking fullbacks, most crosses per 90, strong both short & long.
strengths: Most shots per 90 — a relentless attacking team
Most crosses per 90 from 1v1 wingers & attacking fullbacks
Best long & short passers per 90; excellent defence & rest-defence (best in AFCON, 3rd in CAF qualifying)

keyPlayers.name: Bilal El Khannouss
keyPlayers.note: Exciting, creative attacking mid — pacey, direct dribbler, 9 goal involvements in 19 games, 45 chances created

tactics: Dominate the ball, get the wingers and fullbacks attacking 1v1, and flood the box with crosses, all on top of an excellent defensive base. Hakimi could have headlined, but Bilal may have a breakout moment.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
